LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MONICA MULLEN AND RICKY VIGIL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MARCH 21, 2013. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MONICA MULLEN & RICKY VIGIL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION TO MAKE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING OF THE NON EXISTENCE OF ANY GENUINE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHICH DEFENDANT IS LIABLE FOR THE ASSAULT AND BATTERY. THE PUNITIVE DAMAGE ISSUE CANNOT BE RESOLVED ON SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Hayden S. Alfano, Esq. of Toschi Sidran Collins & Doyle, Tel. 510.835.3400, for defendant and moving party;
Michael G. Thomas, Esq. of Kern Noda Devine & Segal, Tel. 415.474.1900, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: DEFENDANTS MONICA MULLEN & RICKY VIGIL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant has not met its burden of production to make a prima facie showing of the non existence of any genuine issue of material fact. There is a triable issue of material fact as to which defendant is liable for the assault and battery. The punitive damage issue cannot be resolved on summary adjudication. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
